layman_headline: Market fear is screaming 'buy' but the charts are screaming 'wait' — patience wins here.
layman_explanation: The crypto market is in 'Extreme Fear' mode with a score of 25/100, which historically has been a good time to start looking for bargains. However, almost every altcoin candidate on the watchlist has bearish chart structure — prices are below their moving averages with ugly patterns. Bitcoin itself is recovering (+5% this week) but Bitcoin dominance at 56.5% means money is flowing to BTC, not alts. Jumping into weak altcoins during a BTC-dominance phase is a recipe for underperformance.
layman_action: Stay in cash for now. The fear levels suggest a bounce opportunity is forming, but the technical setups aren't clean enough yet. Wait for a confirmed reversal pattern before committing capital — likely within the next few days.
expert_reasoning: The watchlist presents a challenging set of candidates. BONK has a -2.0 chart bias with Three Black Crows and Bearish Marubozu — a falling knife. NIGHT dropped 39% in 7 days with no support levels or volume data. LDO has the best chart bias (+0.85) with Three White Soldiers, but RSI at 59 after a 29% weekly run gives poor risk/reward (R:R ratio only 0.38x to resistance). DEXE is overbought at RSI 71 after a 63% 5-day surge. ETH is the cleanest candidate: RSI at 39 (reliable signal per our track record — 83% win rate), Extreme Fear sentiment (67% win rate signal), and massive $41.5B TVL ecosystem. However, the bearish chart structure (-0.7 bias) and the fact it's already bounced 8% this week means chasing here offers mediocre risk/reward. The disciplined play is to wait for a pullback to ~$1,850 or a breakout above the SMA20 for confirmation.
risk_factors: BTC dominance at 56.5% continues to compress alt performance — even a good ETH setup can underperform
Extreme Fear trending flat (not improving) — sentiment could deteriorate further on any macro shock
All swing candidates show bearish chart structures — catching falling knives in a risk-off environment
watch_list: ETH price action around $1,900 resistance — a daily close above would confirm the bounce and trigger entry
Fear & Greed index movement — a shift from 25 toward 30+ would confirm sentiment bottoming
BTC dominance trend — any decline below 56% would signal capital rotation back to alts
market_context: The broader crypto market is in Extreme Fear (25/100) with BTC recovering but dominance elevated at 56.5%, creating a hostile environment for altcoin swing trades. Most alts remain in bearish structure despite a mild relief bounce this week.
context_banner: Extreme Fear creates opportunity but bearish alt structures demand patience — wait for confirmed reversal before deploying capital.

key_signals.description: Extreme Fear at 25/100 trending flat — historically a contrarian buy zone but not yet showing improvement, suggesting the bottom may not be fully in.
ETH RSI at 39 is approaching oversold territory, a reliable signal (83% win rate in our track record), but chart structure remains bearish with price below all MAs.
BONK shows Three Black Crows and Bearish Marubozu (-2.0 bias), LDO shows Three White Soldiers (+0.85 bias) but is already extended — no clean entry exists across candidates.
ETH derivatives reset article suggests positioning is being unwound, which could set up a cleaner long entry once the flush completes.
Grayscale filing S-1 for Worldcoin ETF and CLARITY bill progress show continued institutional crypto adoption — medium-term bullish backdrop.
